LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MOBILITY PLUS TRANSPORTATION, LLC AND TECTRANS, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ALL CIVIL COURT PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT SHE SIGNED IS SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. THE FAILURE TO EXCLUDE PAGA OR UCL CLAIMS FROM THE AGREEMENT DOES NOT RENDER THE AGREEMENT UNCONSCIONABLE. NOR IS THERE ANY LACK OF MUTUALITY. BROWN IS INAPPOSITE SINCE THAT CASE DEALT WITH A WAIVER OF A PAGA CLAIM AND DID NOT HOLD THAT A PAGA CLAIM COULDN'T BE ARBITRATED. TO THE EXTENT THAT CRUZ HAS ANY CONTINUING VITALITY AFTER CONCEPCION, WHICH IS UNCLEAR AT THE MOMENT, PLAINTIFF'S UCL CLAIM FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF CAN BE SEVERED FROM HER OTHER CLAIMS AND TRIED IN THIS COURT AFTER THE CONCLUSION OF THE ARBITRATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY: PATTISON, ROBERT MALCOLM SUBSTITUTED FOR RAHM, RICHARD HOWARD AS ATTORNEY FOR MOBILITY PLUS TRANSPORTATION, LLC, A CORPORATION

Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay All Civil Court Proceedings
Off Calendar Oct-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS MOBILITY PLUS TRANSPORTATION, LLC AND TECTRANS, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ALL CIVIL COURT PROCEEDINGS.

APPEARANCES: John T. Mullan, Esq. of Rudy Exelrod Zieff & Lowe LLP 394-5597 for pltf & opposing party; Richard H. Rahm, Esq. 677-3104 & Lucas V. Munoz, Esq. 677-3195 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendants' motion to compel arbitration and stay action is granted. Plaintiff has not shown that the arbitration agreement she signed is substantively unconscionable. The failure to exclude PAGA or UCL claims from the agreement does not render the agreement unconscionable. Nor is there any lack of mutuality. Brown is inapposite since that case dealt with a waiver of a PAGA claim and did not hold that a PAGA claim couldn't be arbitrated. To the extent that Cruz has any continuing vitality after Concepcion, which is unclear at the moment, plaintiff's UCL claim for injunctive relief can be severed from her other claims and tried in this court after the conclusion of the arbitration. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
